name: Procrastinate
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); no API costs; requires PostgreSQL

auth.notes: Uses existing PostgreSQL connection; no separate auth

performance.notes: PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY for task delivery; near-real-time without polling

agent_readiness.notes: PostgreSQL-native task queue. Uses LISTEN/NOTIFY for real-time job delivery without Redis/RabbitMQ. ACID-compliant job persistence. asyncio-first. Job scheduling (cron-like). Retry policies. Priority queues. Django/SQLAlchemy ORM integration. Admin interface. No additional infrastructure — just PostgreSQL. Ideal for teams already using PostgreSQL who don't want to add Redis for task queuing.

gotchas: PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY not ideal for very high message throughput
No built-in worker monitoring dashboard (third-party needed)
best_when: Teams using PostgreSQL who want to avoid Redis dependency for task queues
ACID-guaranteed job delivery with transactional consistency
Django/FastAPI apps with PostgreSQL needing async background tasks
avoid_when: Very high throughput messaging (millions of messages/sec — use Redis/Kafka)
Teams needing battle-tested scaling like Celery
summary: Procrastinate is a PostgreSQL-native task queue using LISTEN/NOTIFY for real-time delivery without Redis. ACID-compliant, asyncio-first, with retry policies, priority queues, and cron scheduling. Django/SQLAlchemy integration. Best for teams using PostgreSQL who want to avoid adding Redis just for background tasks.
